title: Charlotte Crime, 1985–2026 — The Flat Line Across 14 Police Divisions
titleOptions: Charlotte Crime Has Been Flat for 8 Years — but the Map Underneath Is Moving
75,042 to 75,179: Eight Years of Charlotte Crime, Mapped Division by Division
description:
Forty years of reported crime in Charlotte — the FBI-era climb to the 2007 peak, the fall after it, and then eight years of CMPD's own count that end almost exactly where they began: 75,042 in 2017, 75,179 in 2025. Underneath that flat citywide line the map keeps moving — University City up 17% since 2022, the Airport division down 33%.

This is a fully sourced, reproducible data visualization. Every figure comes straight from the FBI Uniform Crime Reporting program and the Charlotte-Mecklenburg Police Department's own open incident data. Every dot on the map is a real reported incident location, anonymized to the block by the source. Coverage and caveats are shown on screen.

CMPD's public dataset excludes non-criminal 800-series report types (128,848 records) and reports cleared as unfounded (21,392) — both disclosed, with counts, in the provenance record. The FBI's classic 'index' crimes (through 2016) and CMPD's NIBRS incident reports (2017+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, never merged into one line. The step at the seam is the ruler changing, not a crime wave.

▶ CHAPTERS
0:00  Hook — the flat line
0:08  What you're about to see
0:22  Chapter 1 — the long arc
2:30  The last five years — up close
2:43  Chapter 2 — the map, month by month
4:52  The busiest & safest areas
5:18  Sources & credits

▶ MADE BY AN AI — FULLY TRANSPARENT
This video was produced end-to-end by Claude (Anthropic's AI): it located and fetched the official source data, built and validated the data pipeline, verified every on-screen figure against the sources, designed and rendered the visuals, and generated the music. No human edited the numbers. Every step — code, data, and design decisions — is public and reproducible in the repository linked below. If you find an error, open an issue; corrections are part of the record.

▶ CODE, DATA & FULL SOURCES (reproduce every number)
https://github.com/hadi-nayebi/crime-cartography/tree/main/videos/charlotte-nc

Data: Charlotte-Mecklenburg Police Department / City of Charlotte open data. Music generated with Stable Audio Open (Stability AI). Basemap © OpenStreetMap contributors.

If this was worth a look, please Like, Subscribe, and Share.
